chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the number of seasons they were champions of the national cup under specific conditions. We need to know the German Cup performance for each season. We extract the value from column "german cup" and create a different column "cup performance" for each row. The datatype is String.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
champion of the national cup -> german cup
column value of the question link to columns:
won it every season -> german cup
never have won it at all -> german cup
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
None

The question is asking to determine the number of seasons where they were both champion every season (consistently champion) and also seasons where they never won at all (never champion). Since each row represents a season and the "german cup" column indicates their performance (champion, semifinalist, or not applicable), we need to group by the "german cup" column to analyze performance patterns. We can use the f_group_column(german cup) operation to group the seasons based on their cup status to analyze this situation.

The question is asking for the number of seasons where the team was both champion of the national cup every season and never have won it at all. This is a contradictory condition, as a team cannot be both a champion every season and never have won it at all. Therefore, the answer is 0.

judge_thought: The question asks how many seasons the team was champion of the national cup under the condition that they must have won it every season and also never have won it at all. This is a paradoxical condition because a team cannot simultaneously have won the cup every season and never have won it at all. Therefore, the prediction answer of 0 is correct because such a scenario is impossible under the given conditions.
